reasoning: The 360-day average is 13.333 protests (implying we need 14 or more protests in a 30-day window to resolve YES). Event counts typically follow a right-skewed distribution where the median is below the mean, meaning most random 30-day periods will naturally fall below the average. Furthermore, the 360-day reference period (Feb 2025 - Feb 2026) likely includes an inflated baseline due to intense anti-far-right protests following the early 2025 government coalition formation, whereas activity appears more subdued and routine by early 2026. For the first period (Jan 23 - Feb 21, resolving Feb 22), despite local Akademikerball protests in Graz, the lack of widespread national protest waves suggests it will likely fall short of the elevated 14-protest threshold. For the second period (Feb 15 - Mar 16, resolving Mar 17), the probability is higher due to established annual events that generate multi-city protests, specifically the Ukraine invasion anniversary (Feb 24) and International Women's Day (Mar 8). However, following the rule that uncertainty must increase with horizon, the probability for the March resolution is kept closer to 0.5.
